Based on the provided real-time search results, here are the latest AI news headlines focused on **open-source models and updates**:

---

### 🚀 **Latest Open-Source AI News (as of Feb 28, 2026)**

1. **NVIDIA Announces Alpamayo Family of Open-Source AI Models for Autonomous Vehicles**  
   NVIDIA released the **Alpamayo** family—open AI models, simulation tools, and datasets aimed at accelerating safe, reasoning-based autonomous vehicle development.

2. **NVIDIA Debuts Nemotron 3 Family of Open Models & Agent Tools**  
   New open models tailored for AI agent development, alongside:
   - **Nemotron Agentic Safety Dataset** (real-world telemetry for agent safety)
   - **NeMo RL** open-source libraries for training and evaluation
   - Training datasets and code for specialized agents (reasoning, coding, workflows).

3. **NVIDIA Releases Open Datasets & Tools for Broad AI Development**  
   Includes:
   - Dataset and training code for **Llama Embed Nemotron 8B** (featured on MMTEB leaderboard)
   - Updated **LLM Router** (auto-routing to optimal models)
   - Dataset for **Nemotron Speech ASR** model
   - Resources used by companies like CodeRabbit for AI code reviews.

---

### 🔎 **Where to Find Ongoing Open-Source AI Updates**
- **LLM Stats** (llm-stats.com) – Aggregates hourly updates on open-source LLMs from major labs (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Meta, etc.).
- **Perplexity Spaces** – Recommended on Reddit for custom AI news digests (set to “web + social” sources for latest open-source releases).

---

Want a deeper dive into any of these NVIDIA releases or help setting up a custom open-source AI news tracker? 🛠️📡
What about Image/video generation open source models

Based on the latest search results, here are the standout **open-source image and video generation models** as of early 2026:

---

### 🎥 **Top Open-Source Video Generation Models**

| Model | Key Features | Best For | Notes |
|-------|--------------|----------|-------|
| **Wan 2.2** | Up to 1080p, Hybrid Stream DiT, efficient motion modeling | Cinema-friendly 720p/24 on a single RTX 4090 | Includes a 5B hybrid TI2V variant for efficiency |
| **HunyuanVideo** | 13B parameters, xDiT parallelism, FP8 weights, full OSS toolchain (Diffusers/ComfyUI) | Large-scale, general-purpose T2V/I2V with strong motion | Performance rivals or surpasses top closed-source models |
| **Open-Sora** | Democratizes efficient video production, DiT-based, supports various tasks | Research, customization, fine-tuning on own infrastructure | Part of a broader ecosystem (references Latte, PixArt) |

---

### 🖼️ **Top Open-Source Image Generation Models**

| Model | Key Features | Highlights |
|-------|--------------|------------|
| **MoE Image Model** (Bentoml) | 80B total params, 64 experts (~13B active/token) | Largest open-source image-generation MoE to date |
| **PixArt** | DiT-based text-to-image | Fast, high-quality outputs; often paired with video projects |
| **Flux** | Powerful text-to-image generation | Mentioned alongside PixArt in Open-Sora ecosystem |

---

### 📌 **Key Takeaways**
- **Video**: **HunyuanVideo** and **Wan 2.2** are leading for quality and accessibility, respectively.  
- **Image**: Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architectures (like the 80B model) are pushing scale while keeping inference efficient.  
- **Ecosystems**: Many models integrate with **Diffusers** and **ComfyUI**, and some (like Open-Sora) encourage fine-tuning for custom use cases.

---

### 🔗 **Where to Explore**
- **Hyperstack** & **KDnuggets** for practical model comparisons and hardware guidance.
- **GitHub (hpcaitech/Open-Sora)** for code and community tools.
- **WhiteFiber** for performance benchmarks vs. closed-source models.

Need help setting up a specific model or comparing outputs? Let me know your use case (research, production, hardware constraints)! 🛠️

Based on the provided search results, here’s what **Z-Image** is and why it’s notable in the open-source image generation space:

---

### 🖼️ **What is Z-Image?**
- **Developer:** Alibaba’s Tongyi Lab (Tongyi-MAI)
- **Release Date:** January 27, 2026
- **Model Size:** **6 billion parameters** (efficient vs. 20B–80B rivals)
- **Architecture:** **Scalable Single-Stream Diffusion Transformer (S3-DiT)** — designed for high performance without massive compute.
- **License:** **Open-source** — checkpoints released on Hugging Face & ModelScope.

---

### 🔑 **Key Features & Strengths**
1. **Efficiency**  
   Runs well on consumer hardware (unlike 80B MoE models). Aims to challenge the “scale-at-all-costs” trend.
2. **Creative Image-to-Image**  
   Strong **instruction-following** for precise edits via natural language prompts (e.g., “change the sky to sunset while keeping the building”).
3. **Speed & Quality**  
   Claims **millisecond-generation** for 4K-resolution images (marketing claim; real-world speed depends on hardware).
4. **Research-Backed**  
   Backed by an arXiv paper ([2511.22699](https://arxiv.org/abs/2511.22699)) positioning it against proprietary systems (e.g., Seedream 4.0) and open-source giants (FLUX.2, Hunyuan-Image-3.0).

---

### 🌐 **Where to Access It**
- **GitHub:** [Tongyi-MAI/Z-Image](https://github.com/Tongyi-MAI/Z-Image) (code, models, tutorials)
- **Hugging Face / ModelScope:** Model checkpoints for direct use.
- **Demo/Service:** [z-image.io](https://z-image.io/) (web UI) and [z-image.ai](https://z-image.ai/) (optimized deployment).
- **ComfyUI Integration:** [Z-Image-Turbo workflow](https://docs.comfy.org/tutorials/image/z-image/z-image-turbo) for advanced users.

---

### ⚖️ **How It Compares**
| Model | Params | Strengths | Hardware Needs |
|-------|--------|-----------|----------------|
| **Z-Image** | 6B | Efficient, strong I2I, open | **Consumer-grade** (e.g., RTX 4090) |
| **FLUX.2** | ~12B+ | High fidelity, text adherence
